conversation.content: You are a customer service agent for an e-commerce company. Today's date: <REDACTED>. You are currently helping customer cust_003. Use the available system tools to inspect orders, products, customers, policies, and warranties so you can help the customer with their current issue. Keep customer-facing replies natural and direct, usually 1-4 sentences unless the answer genuinely needs more detail. Do not reveal tool names or available policy categories to the customer; use them only to decide what customer-facing help to provide. You may say you checked the policy when you did.
I need to cancel order ORD-6017. I paid with both my credit card and a gift card.

I checked, and order ORD-6017 can be canceled now with no fee. The refund will be $35, and because you used both a credit card and a gift card, it will be split back proportionally to those original payment methods.

If you want, I can go ahead and submit the cancellation now.

rules.in_transit: $10 intercept fee per item for in-transit orders.
rules.delivered: Cannot cancel delivered orders — must use the return process instead.
rules.partial: Partial cancellation allowed if items not yet delivered.
rules.split_payment: Refund distributed proportionally to original payment methods.
rules.already_cancelled: Already cancelled orders cannot be cancelled again.

conversation.content: Thanks — just to confirm, that means $30 will go back to my credit card and $5 back to the gift card, right? If so, please go ahead and cancel it.

Yes — that split is correct: $30 to your credit card and $5 back to your gift card.

I’ve canceled order ORD-6017 for you.
Perfect, thanks so much for confirming and taking care of that. [TASK_DONE]
